pattern: An in-order walk of a binary search tree visits values in ascending order, so the kth value it yields is the answer.
hints: Think about which traversal of a BST produces its values already sorted.
An in-order traversal (left subtree, node, right subtree) emits values smallest-first, so you only need to count visits until you reach the kth.
Walk in order and decrement k at each node; when k hits zero you are standing on the answer, so you can stop early instead of traversing the whole tree.

edge_inputs.description: smallest element of a tiny BST
third smallest in a left-skewed-ish BST
single node, k equal to node count
right-leaning chain — kth value is not the kth node visited in pre-order
k equal to n returns the maximum

stress.description: random valid BST built by inserting a shuffled permutation, k anywhere in range
stress.note: values are a permutation of 0..n-1 so all are distinct and within range
